text:
Olympics



French president Emmanuel Macron says “we are going to do everything” to ensure that European clubs, including Real Madrid, allow players to be available for theParis Olympicsthis summer.

France are keen to put on “a great show” when they host the football tournament at the Games and are hopeful of having a number of high-profile players, including Kylian Mbappe, Aurelien Tchouameni and Eduardo Camavinga, involved.

Advertisement

That will require their clubs playing ball, however, with the competition starting on July 24 and running to August 9 — a period where many teams’ pre-season programmes will already be underway.

TheEuropean Championshipin Germany ends just 10 days prior on July 14, raising concerns over player welfare and an extremely high workload during the traditional offseason.

Clubs can’t be compelled to release players for theOlympics, presenting the possibility of difficult conversations between now and Paris.

“We are going to do everything,” Macron toldRMC Sport. “I have confidence in the French and European clubs, if I may say so if you see what I mean.

“We have the Euro (Euro 2024) before, that’s the problem. I think the clubs have to play their part if we want to have a great show.”

Mbappe, meanwhile, has previously said it would be a “dream” to compete at an Olympics, witha concerted push from the likes of the French Football Federation (FFF), French politicians and Paris-Saint Germain team-mates already well underwayto try and enable him to participate.

Where Mbappe will be playing his domestic football by then remains to be confirmed.Real Madrid appear to be leading the way to sign him, with his contract at PSG set to expire in June.

Asked if he was referring to Real (where Tchouameni and Camavinga already play), Macron added: “Exactly. When I was speaking about European clubs, I was speaking about them.

“The players need to take it into account in their preparations and their training sessions too because we have the Euros and I hope we’ll be playing until July 14.

“I hope and I think we will. I haven’t spoken about it with Mbappe but I hope that his club will let him.”

Real Madrid, Mbappe and the Olympics: 'We'll go and meet Florentino if we have to'

(Kirill Kudryavtsev/AFP via Getty Images)

Ben Burrowsis Managing Editor, News for The Athletic based in London. Prior to joining in 2023 he was Sports Editor at The Independent.Follow Ben on Twitter@benburrows_
title: Emmanuel Macron puts pressure on clubs to release players for Olympics: ‘We are going to do everything’ - The Athletic
author: Ben Burrows
description:
